fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: South Dakota Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to South Dakota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity south dakota, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to south dakota, pharmacy technician compact south dakota, south dakota pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: South Dakota Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: 4303 S. Louise Ave., Suite 204, Sioux Falls, SD 57106-3156

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in South Dakota.

steps.title: 1. Create an eLicense Account
steps.description: Navigate to the eLicense South Dakota portal at https://elicense.sd.gov. Click on 'Register for an Account' and follow the prompts to create your new user profile.

steps.title: 2. Start New Application
steps.description: Log in to your eLicense account. From the dashboard, select 'Apply for a New License/Permit' and choose 'Pharmacy Technician New Application' under the South Dakota Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: 3. Complete Application & Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form. This includes personal details, educational history, and answering attestation questions. Submit the non-refundable application fee of $35.00 through the portal.

steps.title: 4. Submit Fingerprints for Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within the eLicense application or on the Board's website (https://doh.sd.gov/boards/pharmacy/forms.aspx) for completing the fingerprint-based criminal background check. Schedule an appointment with IdentoGO and use the South Dakota Board of Pharmacy Service Code (151W03) for processing.

steps.title: 5. Await Board Review and Registration
steps.description: Ensure all parts of your application, including the background check results, have been submitted. The South Dakota Board of Pharmacy will review your complete application and, upon approval, issue your Pharmacy Technician registration.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a South Dakota pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does South Dakota have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in South Dakota. To work in South Dakota, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to South Dakota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in South Dakota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in South Dakota
hero.dek: Apply online through the eLicense South Dakota portal (elicense.sd.gov). Create an account, search for the 'Pharmacy Technician New Application', and follow the prompts to submit your application and background check.
